source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Irene made a "city" using identical wooden cubes. We have, beside, a view from above and a side view of this "city". We do not know which side of the "city" is being shown. What is the smallest amount of cubes Irene may have used to make its assembly?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly references 'a view from above and a side view' of the cube assembly
determining the minimum number of cubes depends on interpreting visual projections and spatial structure
multi_step: the task combines at least two visual constraints: top view and side view
question_text adds uncertainty with 'We do not know which side of the city is being shown'
finding the 'smallest amount of cubes' requires optimization under the projection constraints rather than a direct lookup
verifiability: the result is in principle checkable by reconstructing a cube arrangement consistent with both views and confirming minimality

decision_reasons: assets are complete: question, image, and numeric answer are all present and registry passed
initial scoring is strong, with high image dependency, solid multi-step reasoning, and good verifiability
although image-based verification and side-view orientation ambiguity increase review difficulty, the core semantics are well supported by the available assets
